description: Real-device pilot summary (paper section 'Real-Device Pilot'). 14-day IRB-approved deployment in notification-only mode (no revocation) on 35 volunteer devices. Raw traces were pseudonymized on-device, aggregated, and deleted at study end, so this study is NOT re-runnable from released artifacts; this file records the reported aggregate outcomes.

protocol.mode: notification-only (detection/alerting pathway only; enforcement policy not field-tested)
protocol.irb: approved; number withheld for anonymous review
protocol.false_alert_adjudication: alert counted false if participant marked it 'expected behavior' OR two authors, blind to model score, independently judged the use consistent with declared functionality

key_findings: The 3.4% field false-alert rate exceeds the 2.5% simulated budget: the constraint does not transfer unmodified (a distinct construct from simulated FRR — human-adjudicated, enforcement disabled).
Deployment requires ~72-hour on-device dual recalibration with revocation enabled; eps_safe = 0.04 offered only as a starting point.
Latency gap vs simulation stems from AppOpsManager background execution limits; at field latencies the practical lever is rate limiting rather than post-hoc revocation.
